title: Multiply matrices by scalars
description: Practice your understanding of the process of multiplying a matrix by a scalar.

hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-4 & 12
\\\\
16 & 4
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
2 & -6
\\\\
-8 & -4
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
12 & -6
\\\\
-3 & 12
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-5 & 0
\\\\
10 & 5
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-2 & 3
\\\\
1 & 0
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-1 & 2
\\\\
1 & -3
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
3 & -1
\\\\
-2 & 4
\end{array}\right]$
To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

This is the product:

$\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-5 & 1
\\\\
-4 & 3
\end{array}\right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$\dfrac12\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
x & -2 \\\\
0 & y \\\\
-6 & 2 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
2 & -1 \\\\
0 & 4 \\\\
-3 & 1
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=4
\\\\
y&=8
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$-\dfrac13\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
x
\\\\
-9
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
2
\\\\
y
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-6
\\\\
y&=3
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$\dfrac15\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
0 & x \\\\
20 & y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
0 & 6 \\\\
4 & -3
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=30
\\\\
y&=-15
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$-\dfrac14\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
x & 12 & -32
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
5 & -3 & y
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-20
\\\\
y&=8
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$3\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
1 & 0 & x
\\\\
y & -1 & 4
\\\\
-3 & 5 & 1 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
3 & 0 & -9
\\\\
6 & -3 & 12
\\\\
-9 & 15 & 3
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-3
\\\\
y&=2
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following scalar multiplication.**

$-2\cdot\left[ \begin{array}{c}
5 & 1 & -3
\\\\
x & 2 & y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{c}
-10 & -2 & 6
\\\\
8 & -4 & 0
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To multiply a matrix by a scalar, we multiply each term of the matrix by the scalar.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-4
\\\\
y&=0
\end{align}$

\\\\
x
\\\\

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

\\\\

\end{array} \right]
